feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Il modo più classico per fare gli auguri.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice 'Buon compleanno!'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The most classic way to wish a happy birthday.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say 'Happy birthday!'.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì, come nella canzone!
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la frase della canzone tipica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes, just like in the song!
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the phrase from the typical song.

english: A hundred of these days! (May you have many more!)

feedback.correctItalian: Un augurio molto comune in Italia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa per augurare lunga vita e felicità.
feedback.correctEnglish: A very common wish in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's used to wish long life and happiness.

english: Thanks a lot for the thought!
choicesEnglish: Thanks a lot for the thought!

feedback.correctItalian: Una risposta gentile agli auguri.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si ringrazia per il pensiero avuto.
feedback.correctEnglish: A kind response to birthday wishes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're thanking for the thought someone had.

choicesEnglish: How many gifts do you have?

feedback.correctItalian: Un modo informale per chiedere l'età.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede quanti anni compie oggi.
feedback.correctEnglish: An informal way to ask someone's age.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking how many years they turn today.

feedback.correctItalian: Si risponde spesso così indicando il numero.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si usa il verbo essere per indicare gli anni compiuti.
feedback.correctEnglish: It's often answered this way by stating the number.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the verb 'essere' is used to indicate the years completed.

feedback.correctItalian: Una battuta classica di chi compie gli anni.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo scherzoso di lamentarsi dell'età.
feedback.correctEnglish: A classic joke from the birthday person.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a playful way to complain about age.

english: No way! You are very young!
choicesEnglish: No way! You are very young!

feedback.correctItalian: Un complimento per rassicurare il festeggiato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si nega l'idea che l'altra persona sia vecchia.
feedback.correctEnglish: A compliment to reassure the birthday person.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're denying the idea that the other person is old.

feedback.correctItalian: Si usa molto nei messaggi di auguri.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo caloroso di chiudere un messaggio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Very commonly used in birthday messages.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a warm way to end a message.

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede quali siano i piani per il compleanno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si domanda come si intende celebrare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking what the birthday plans are.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking how they intend to celebrate.

english: I'm having a party on Saturday.
choicesEnglish: I'm having a party on Saturday.
I'm going to the movies on Saturday.

feedback.correctItalian: Sì, l'annuncio dei festeggiamenti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, comunica l'intenzione di dare una festa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Yes, announcing the celebrations.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it communicates the intention to throw a party.

choicesEnglish: I hope you have a gift!

feedback.correctItalian: Un invito caloroso a un amico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprime il desiderio che l'altro partecipi.
feedback.correctEnglish: A warm invitation to a friend.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it expresses the desire for the other to participate.

feedback.correctItalian: Il momento clou della festa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si invita tutti ad alzare il bicchiere.
feedback.correctEnglish: The highlight of the party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're inviting everyone to raise their glass.

feedback.correctItalian: L'azione prima di mangiare la torta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice al festeggiato di soffiare.
feedback.correctEnglish: The action before eating the cake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you tell the birthday person to blow.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice sempre prima di soffiare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'invito a pensare a qualcosa che si vuole.
feedback.correctEnglish: Always said before blowing.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the invitation to think of something you want.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice consegnando il presente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, accompagna il gesto di dare il regalo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Said while handing over the present.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it accompanies the gesture of giving the gift.

feedback.correctItalian: La curiosità degli amici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'esortazione a scartare il regalo.
feedback.correctEnglish: The friends' curiosity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the urge to unwrap the gift.

choicesEnglish: I hope it's enough for you.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice spesso quando si fa un regalo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si spera che il dono sia gradito.
feedback.correctEnglish: Often said when giving a gift.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you hope the gift is appreciated.

feedback.correctItalian: Una reazione entusiasta al regalo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il modo per mostrare apprezzamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: An enthusiastic reaction to the gift.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the way to show appreciation.

feedback.correctItalian: La domanda tipica davanti a un pacchetto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede il contenuto del regalo.
feedback.correctEnglish: The typical question in front of a package.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking about the content of the gift.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice per cortesia quando si riceve un regalo impegnativo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un'espressione di modesta sorpresa e ringraziamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Said out of politeness when receiving a significant gift.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's an expression of modest surprise and thanks.

feedback.correctItalian: Un augurio classico e sincero.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sono le due cose che si augurano più spesso.
feedback.correctEnglish: A classic and sincere wish.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, they are the two things most often wished for.

feedback.correctItalian: Dalla conversazione: ricevere tantissimi auguri.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa aver ricevuto moltissime comunicazioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: From the conversation: receiving tons of wishes.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means having received very many communications.

feedback.correctItalian: Un modo per rincuorare chi invecchia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice che gli anni che si compiono siano i più belli.
feedback.correctEnglish: A way to hearten someone getting older.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're saying the years they're turning are the most beautiful.

feedback.correctItalian: L'invito a vivere appieno la giornata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esorta a trarre tutto il piacere possibile.
feedback.correctEnglish: The invitation to fully live the day.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it urges to take all possible pleasure.

english: Who is coming to the party?
choicesItalian: A che ora è la festa?
choicesEnglish: Who is coming to the party?
What are you bringing to the party?
Where are you having the party?

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede degli altri invitati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si domanda l'identità degli ospiti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking about the other guests.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking the identity of the guests.

feedback.correctItalian: Una risposta tipica per una festa grande.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che la cerchia di amici sarà presente.
feedback.correctEnglish: A typical response for a big party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the circle of friends will be present.

feedback.correctItalian: Il momento più atteso dai golosi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'invito a dividere il dolce.
feedback.correctEnglish: The most awaited moment for those with a sweet tooth.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the invitation to divide the dessert.

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede per servire da bere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si offre lo spumante per il brindisi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking in order to serve drinks.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're offering sparkling wine for the toast.

feedback.correctItalian: Il brindisi collettivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'esclamazione che accompagna il brindisi.
feedback.correctEnglish: The collective toast.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the exclamation that accompanies the toast.

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede chi abbia portato il pacchetto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si domanda chi sia il mittente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking who brought the package.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking who the sender is.

feedback.correctItalian: Quando un regalo è fatto da un gruppo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che il dono è collettivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: When a gift is given by a group.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the gift is collective.

feedback.correctItalian: Un ringraziamento caloroso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sottolinea la gentilezza del gesto.
feedback.correctEnglish: A warm thank you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it emphasizes the kindness of the gesture.

feedback.correctItalian: L'invito a divertirsi alla festa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si propone di ballare.
feedback.correctEnglish: The invitation to have fun at the party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're proposing to dance.

feedback.correctItalian: Quando la festa entra nel vivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede di aumentare il suono.
feedback.correctEnglish: When the party gets lively.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking to increase the sound.

feedback.correctItalian: Informazione logistica fondamentale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede l'orario di inizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Fundamental logistical information.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for the start time.

feedback.correctItalian: Un saluto prima di trovarsi alla festa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un congedo temporaneo.
feedback.correctEnglish: A greeting before meeting at the party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a temporary farewell.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice scherzosamente a un amico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che l'orario previsto è passato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Said jokingly to a friend.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that the scheduled time has passed.

feedback.correctItalian: Una scusa comune per il ritardo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, giustifica un contrattempo.
feedback.correctEnglish: A common excuse for being late.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it justifies a mishap.

english: The important thing is that you're here.
choicesEnglish: The important thing is that you're here.
The important thing is the gift.
The important thing is the cake.
The important thing is the party.

feedback.correctItalian: Una risposta accogliente dell'ospite.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, rassicura l'amico che è arrivato tardi.
feedback.correctEnglish: A welcoming response from the host.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it reassures the friend who arrived late.

feedback.correctItalian: Un complimento alla festeggiata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si loda l'aspetto dell'altra persona.
feedback.correctEnglish: A compliment to the birthday person.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're praising the other person's appearance.

feedback.correctItalian: Si risponde così a un complimento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un modo modesto di ringraziare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Responding this way to a compliment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a modest way of thanking.

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede l'autore del dolce.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si domanda chi l'abbia cucinata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking the author of the dessert.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking who cooked it.

english: I got it at the pastry shop.
choicesEnglish: I got it at the pastry shop.
I found it on the street.
I had it as a gift.

feedback.correctItalian: Significa che è stata comprata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la provenienza del dolce.
feedback.correctEnglish: It means it was bought.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the origin of the dessert.

feedback.correctItalian: Un complimento al cibo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, squisita significa molto buona.
feedback.correctEnglish: A compliment to the food.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'squisita' means very good.

feedback.correctItalian: Si offre il bis della torta.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si propone un'altra piccola porzione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Offering a second helping of cake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're proposing another small portion.

feedback.correctItalian: Si rifiuta gentilmente il cibo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa non aver più fame.
feedback.correctEnglish: Kindly refusing food.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means no longer being hungry.

feedback.correctItalian: Si chiede un bilancio del compleanno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si domanda come sia trascorso il giorno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Asking for a summary of the birthday.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking how the day was spent.

feedback.correctItalian: Dalla conversazione: averne ricevuti tantissimi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa aver ricevuto moltissimi doni.
feedback.correctEnglish: From the conversation: having received very many.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means having received very many presents.

feedback.correctItalian: La domanda finale dopo la festa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si chiede se l'esperienza sia stata piacevole.
feedback.correctEnglish: The final question after the party.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking if the experience was pleasant.

feedback.correctItalian: Un ringraziamento finale agli amici.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, conferma il divertimento e ringrazia.
feedback.correctEnglish: A final thank you to friends.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it confirms the fun and gives thanks.

english: Whose turn is it to blow?
choicesEnglish: Whose turn is it to blow?
Whose turn is it to cut?
Whose turn is it to drink?
Whose turn is it to dance?

feedback.correctItalian: Ovviamente al festeggiato!
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si scherza su chi deve spegnere le candeline.
feedback.correctEnglish: Obviously the birthday person!
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a joke about who should put out the candles.

feedback.correctItalian: Per ricordare la serata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si invita tutti a mettersi in posa.
feedback.correctEnglish: To remember the evening.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're inviting everyone to pose.

feedback.correctItalian: Istruzioni per la foto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice prima di scattare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Instructions for the photo.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's said before taking the shot.

feedback.correctItalian: Si usa anche in Italia per le foto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la parola per sorridere in foto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Also used in Italy for photos.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the word to smile for photos.

choicesEnglish: What a long party, too much.

feedback.correctItalian: Un apprezzamento sincero dell'ospite.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, conferma che l'evento è riuscito bene.
feedback.correctEnglish: A sincere appreciation from the guest.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it confirms that the event went well.

english: I'm happy you guys like it.
choicesEnglish: I'm happy you guys like it.
I'm sad you guys like it.
I'm tired you guys like it.
I'm sure you guys like it.

feedback.correctItalian: La soddisfazione di chi ha organizzato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprime piacere per l'apprezzamento altrui.
feedback.correctEnglish: The satisfaction of the one who organized.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it expresses pleasure at others' appreciation.

feedback.correctItalian: Si propone di bere ancora insieme.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si suggerisce un nuovo brindisi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Proposing to drink together again.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're suggesting a new toast.

feedback.correctItalian: Si dice quando si toccano i bicchieri.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è l'augurio classico brindando.
feedback.correctEnglish: Said when glasses touch.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the classic wish while toasting.

feedback.correctItalian: Un modo informale per brindare a qualcuno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si intende 'alla tua salute'.
feedback.correctEnglish: An informal way to toast someone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'to your health'.
